猜想.摘要: Round-budget closure at round 20/20 keeps 'ICT' ahead of 'TICT'. The main unresolved gap remains: Closure justification for ICT versus TICT remains missing. Because rounds_remaining_including_current is 1, there is no budget for a new follow-up pairwise task in this stage.

最终证据摘要: Top1 is ICT and top2 is TICT. The latest evidence strengthens the current top1 modestly because the fresh-bundle direct parse again failed to produce any ESIPT-supporting geometry/contact observables, leaving ICT unchallenged. The top2 challenger TICT is essentially unaffected by the latest evidence and remains only a residual runner-up because it was already directly screened by torsion-sensitive evidence. An additional previously credible hypothesis, ESIPT, no longer remains unresolved coverage debt on process grounds: at the final round it should be marked blocked_by_capability after repeated supported internal attempts failed to expose the required scaffold-specific preorganization readout. The case therefore legitimately leaves portfolio screening and enters pairwise contraction. The key remaining discriminator is not broad portfolio coverage anymore but closure-quality justification for ICT versus TICT, while noting ESIPT has been dispositioned as capability-blocked and strongly weakened on the merits. Because this is the final round and verifier supplementation is already sufficient while closure justification for the current pair is still missing, the best next action is no new agent call; instead the system should record that pairwise contraction is now warranted but not fully closed within the remaining budget.

Round-budget closure at round 20/20 keeps 'ICT' ahead of 'TICT'. The main unresolved gap remains: Closure justification for ICT versus TICT remains missing. Because rounds_remaining_including_current is 1, there is no budget for a new follow-up pairwise task in this stage.

External verifier supplementation is already sufficient for ICT-versus-ESIPT family-level comparison: it clarifies ESIPT structural requirements and generally weakens ESIPT for this scaffold class, but it does not replace molecule-specific internal disposition. That internal disposition is now concluded as capability-blocked.
A dedicated TICT-screening task was previously completed and favored ICT, but no final closure-grade pairwise synthesis was produced within the round budget.

Round 20 of 20 has exhausted the portfolio-screening budget. The latest direct parse on the fresh round-18 baseline bundle again failed to recover ESIPT-relevant O-H to candidate N geometry/contact observables. Given the repeated supported internal attempts and no remaining rounds for another changed route, ESIPT is now best classified as blocked by capability for direct molecule-specific internal screening. Portfolio screening is therefore complete by capability blockade rather than by direct positive/negative geometric adjudication.

Internal capabilities were sufficient for donor-acceptor screening, baseline bundles, torsion snapshots, and preorganization screening, but not for reliably extracting the specific phenolic O-H to candidate N observables needed to directly adjudicate ESIPT on this scaffold. At the round limit, the correct process disposition is capability blockade rather than another indirect repeat. Round budget exhausted at round 20/20; no additional specialized-agent execution can be scheduled beyond this point.

Latest microscopic evidence: exact execution of extract_geometry_descriptors_from_bundle on round_18_baseline_bundle reused existing artifacts but still did not return phenolic O-H donor geometry, candidate O-N/H-N distances, relative O-H...N orientation, six-membered-ring compatibility, or ESIPT preorganization compatibility. This further weakens ESIPT on the merits and establishes practical capability blockage for direct internal screening.
